analysis_description: Round 3 tapes days 0–2: TTE at slice open = 8,7,6 days per round3description.txt (CSV day d → 8−d). Black–Scholes implied vol from mids (r=0, T=TTE/365). Analytical BS delta per VEV leg from those IVs (means in summary CSV). Rolling z-score (500 ticks) of mid butterfly C5000+C5200−2·C5100; conditional mean next-tick bf change when z>threshold (grid 1.5–3.0).
used_to_decide: High z associates with negative next bf delta on tape (convexity overstated), motivating short-fly fade; monotone vertical on mids holds (no literal arb violations). Chose z entry grid and shorter window in v3 after v0 crossing cost and v1 bump filter blocking volume.

analysis_description: New IV/Greek-conditioned event study on ROUND_3 days 0-2 (same TTE mapping from round3description: day d -> 8-d at open). For windows 300/400/500 and z thresholds 1.8/2.0/2.2/2.4, measured conditional next-tick butterfly change with IV-bump filters; also segmented by BS delta(5100). Best stability came from window=400 and z around 2.2 with no strict bump gate in execution.
analysis_outputs: terminal event-study output in this run (win/z/bump grid)
used_to_decide: Grid showed stronger mean reversion for higher z but sparse samples when over-filtering by IV bump/delta. Selected v4 params Z_HI=2.2, Z_LO=0.8, BF_WINDOW=400, CLIP=10; kept passive one-tick pricing from v3.

analysis_description: Vega thread on ROUND_3 tapes (TTE = 8−d days at open per round3description): at each tick compute BS IV from mids, then BS vega per leg; net vega of a short fly is −vega(5000)−vega(5200)+2·vega(5100) (positive in sample). For rolling z (window 400) > 2.2, report mean net vega: day0 ~50.0, day1 ~65.3, day2 ~74.2 (higher TTE day has larger body vega). Backtester grid on v4 code: (2.15,0.8,400,10) total 182 vs v4 124; wider window 450 or Z_LO 0.85 hurt.
analysis_outputs: one-off vega table in analysis.json iteration 5
backtester grid: lower_entry 2.15/0.8/400/10 = 182; v4 baseline 124; w450 = -138; Z_LO 0.85 = -4
used_to_decide: Picked v5: lower Z_HI to 2.15 to capture a bit more of the tape’s high-z episodes without changing window/exit; leaves execution identical to v4.
